description: Want to send a message to a friend? Lover? Mysterious stranger with a cool aura? Come visit Message in a Bottle, SOAK's three-dimensional missed connections. Paper and writing utensils are provided for message-making, once your message is complete, roll it up and place it in an empty bottle. Write the recipient's name and/or description on the bottle tag and leave for them to find the next time they wander by. Check back in throughout the weekend to see if anything has been left for you!

description:
Open throughout the event. Look for the starburst lights (which are not for taking!)
 Did you forget that small something that you need? We might have it! This is a whimsical, resource-sharing project that is operated on the honor system, and designed to foster community kindness, sustainability, and mutual support. Take what you need, leave what you can.

Take What You Need: Items are free to take, but please limit to just a couple of items per visit
Leave What You Can: Contributors are encouraged to leave clean, working items/swag - this helps keep things going!
Keep it Neatly Stocked: Please keep the chest tidy, after exploring - refolding clothing or putting items back in bags they started in.
Prohibited Items: Please no dirty, broken, or dangerous items are allowed.

Items initially seeded in the chest include stickers, clothing, USB cables, audio adapters, books, BDSM adjacent items, sunglasses, drink ware, bags, burn swag, and several other
title: MzFit’s Magical Treasure Chest (PG-13+)

We are very excited to share it with our SOAK community!

description: Delicious coffee served in a cool space with extraordinary hospitality from 8-noon Burner Standard Time. Fresh ground French press dark roast brewed black as the depths of interstellar space itself (BYO creamer if you must adulterate). The hangout for quasi-legal alien beatniks, mutant cyborg gear heads, trans-solar scooter gangs, radical anarchist raypunks, neuroatypical geeks, freaked-out psychonauts, and caffeine fiends of every stripe.

description:
You've heard of Irish Coffee, right? We'd like to bring to you alcoholic (and NA, should you prefer) caffeinated concoctions from cultures around the world!

Do you need a Caffè Corretto from Italy, or perhaps a Carajillo (in either Spanish or Mexican style), or maybe even a Karsk from Norway?

Try these drinks as well as many others from around the world every morning of SOAK.

description: Calling all critters, cute or feral! Whether you’re a nocturnal beast refusing to sleep or a songbird greeting the dawn, it’s time for a watering hole gathering. Don your ears, horns, or scales and join the pack for a caffeine migration.

description: Pour Over Grits is a morning pour of indie-coated, southern-fried, funky folk, stirred together with buttery gospel and soul. From British emulators to retromodern country and northwestern sounds, it’s music for the hour when the sun starts warming your tent, stove-tops fire up, coffee drips, and camp slowly comes to life.

description: KBUTT’s Morning Show for folks that have a hard time with transitioning to the waking hours. DJ Yoon, the host of this program, is naturally a night owl who isn’t typically ambulant before noon. Out at SOAK, he can't sleep in so much... This is an eclectic 1-hour radio program for those of you who tend to brew up coffee and eat breakfast well after your peers have already had theirs. It is presented in a classic “AM-Gold” fashion complete with weather updates, local news, and a traffic report.

Bring your own rope if you have some, we will have a limited amount for participants to use.

Or, engage in a fierce Surrealist Debate: absurd arguments in a tournament style bracket. You've discussed Cats vs. Dogs and Science vs. Art before -- but what about Cats vs. Science? Come see what concept goes all the way!

description: We are a silly interactive medical spoof camp, who's motto is "Malpractice Makes Perfect." And we just keep trying! Come "play doctor" with us! It's lots of fun for all ages. Discover your secret wellness needs! Our practitioners offer years of experience that must be atoned.

description:
Thursday - Sunday DanceSafe will have volunteers available for substance checking between hours of 1pm-5pm, we will post any extra hours volunteers will be available on a white board by our booth.

The booth of supplies will be stocked 24/7 though so that people can come get supplies as needed throughout the duration of SOAK.  Stop by our booth at any time to pick up harm reduction supplies such as safer sex supplies, consent literature, safer use kits, drug education and literature, earplugs, fentanyl test strips and more.
Our volunteers will be around the listed times to provide substance checking as well as helping answer any questions you may have about harm reduction and safer use.
title: DanceSafe Substance Verification

description: Hippocampe is not to scale! On its frontage, a uniquely featured giant ear provides a portal into the limbic system of the brain where you can playfully and expressively interact with your brain's left and right hemispheres. Climb through the ear, slide into the brain, and walk into the interactivity areas to experience functions of the left hippocampus, through narrative and memory recall, as well functions of the right hemisphere, through intentional spatial and somatic focus.

Colorful modeling clay, armature wire, guidance, and emotional support will be provided.

description:
This is the yearly training for all Fire Safety volunteers. If you are signed up, great! If not, also great! Come on by, sign up, do four hours of training, and if you like it - you can sign up for volunteer shifts (volunteer shifts happen 24 hours a day, and run from Wednesday morning to Tuesday morning). 

Bring water, wear sturdy shoes. Snacks are a good idea, and clothes that can handle being wet are recommended. 

Topics include propane art inspection, fire hose operations, emergency response driving, pump operations, radio procedures, wildfire theory, and whatever else occurs to us as we chat for the afternoon.
title: Firefighter training

Eat as many tacos as you want.
One at a time...

description:
We’re doing it fancy. We’re doing it wrong. served by people who believe—deeply—that they belong in a much nicer establishment.
Cocktails shaken to order. Bright citrus, a genuinely good non-alcoholic option, and a secret special for the first 30 who show up early and commit to poor decisions.
This is an upscale dinner built entirely on confidence and absolutely no credentials.
It might feel organized. It isn’t.
Eat well. Drink something. Stay a while.
Ask fewer questions.
title: Surf, Turf, and Poor Decisions

camp: The Glory Hole - A Spectacular Boutique
description:
Kick off the weekend at the Glory Hole with cocktails, music, good people, and a comfortable place to land after arrival. Whether you’re fresh through the gate or already fully in event mode, come grab a drink, meet some neighbors, and settle into the weekend with us.

We’ll be slinging cocktails along with quality NA options in a relaxed lounge atmosphere built for conversation, connection, and easing into the magic of the event.

We’ll also be accepting donations for the boutique! If you have clothing, costumes, accessories, faux fur, hats, sparkle, sunglasses, or other fabulous items looking for a new life, bring them by and help keep the Glory Hole spectacular.

Come thirsty. Come shiny. Come hang out.
location: The Glory Hole - A Spectacular Boutique

description:
Open 24 hours a day from Thursday night through Sunday night.

We know you want to learn to lockpick. Pick up this 1337, sweet hacker skill and brag to your friends. Our comfy, chill lockpicking lounge is open all day and night even when the rest of the camp is closed for you to teach yourself how to pick a padlock. Even if you are intimidated just come mess around or watch people try. We will provide cuddly seating, books, tools and resources to learn from, but we do not actively teach this skill. Radical self-reliance!

IMPORTANT LOCATION INFO: We are located at the SOAK Welcome Sign at the end of Forest Entry closest to where you drive in. Most map resources are incorrect about our placement. Don’t let that stop you!

Come join her as we kick off SOAK with her birthday celebration. Popcorn, snacks and kid friendly beverages available.  

If you can't make it to the gathering you can always share your love throughout the event anytime you see her doing laps around please scream HAPPY BIRTHDAY ALICE and watch her smile grow!

description: Colon cancer rates are rising sharply among people under 50, making colon health education more urgent than ever. As an MD/PhD team, we are committed to translating medical knowledge into embodied, accessible experience at our favorite community event. Join us at the inflatable colon on the first evening of SOAK for a quick talk on colon health and Q&A!

Hours may vary from the schedule set here, if we're not home, please swing by again later on.  If the fire is lit, you are welcome to stop by.

description:
As the night tide rises, it may wash you to rocky shores where an ancient creature sleeps. All that remains is a turtle shell and two sleeping eggs; who knows what may slumber within them. Feel yourself grow smaller as you approach this massive Cretaceous relic of the deep past. Crawl inside and grow in size, it’s a tiny cinema! Inside the hollow of the shell is a cozy sanctuary where the festival’s roar fades into the rhythmic hum of the universe. Settle into the landscape of blankets and pillows, gaze up at the screen, and contemplate the mystery and beauty of the natural world. Turtles are radically self-reliant, you know, carrying their shelter on their back. So breathe deeply, you’re home. And once you've caught your breath, you'll emerge, like a hatchling, back into the neon sea. 

Always wanted to ride a giant turtle? Climb aboard and enjoy the view!

description: Gather 'round for a whimsical fire & flow adventure where a camping trip spins delightfully out of control. Tiny props grow magically, bubbles burst into flame, and mischievous sparks lead to dazzling displays of poi, fans, dragon staff, leviwand, fire eating, breathing, and more. Blending playful comedy, physical theater, and high-energy circus arts, this immersive spectacle transforms campsite chaos into a magical night of flame, wonder, and larger-than-life surprises.

description: Join the Cats in Space Fire Circle after dark in an ancient ritual of fire that transcends time and space. Bring a blanket or camp chair to enjoy the show. Fire performers welcome - we provide experienced fire safeties on duty all night, fuel depot, and a dedicated performance space (bring your own props). Fire breathers and specialty props must check in with our safety team before performing. Fuel donations gratefully accepted. All participants must follow posted fire circle guidelines and safety team instructions.

A simple lad from Wall? A fiery haired princess who defies her evil mother to save Elora Danon and the world? Timespace is wibbly wobbly, expect liberties to be taken.

How to play: 1) Explore the SOAK universe while keeping your eyes peeled and your senses sharp! 2) Find a GIANT bean and take this keepsake home with you! No need to return to us. This year we have hidden several bean varieties including Great Northern, Colorful, smaller mutant beans and a few magic beans from last year. 3) Find another bean? Please leave it be so others can discover and participate in the hunt.  The beans will be hidden by 3pm on Friday. All unfound beans will be picked up by end of day Sunday. Happy hunting!

description:
Open throughout the event. Look for the starburst lights (which are not for taking!)
 Did you forget that small something that you need? We might have it! This is a whimsical, resource-sharing project that is operated on the honor system, and designed to foster community kindness, sustainability, and mutual support. Take what you need, leave what you can.

Take What You Need: Items are free to take, but please limit to just a couple of items per visit
Leave What You Can: Contributors are encouraged to leave clean, working items/swag - this helps keep things going!
Keep it Neatly Stocked: Please keep the chest tidy, after exploring - refolding clothing or putting items back in bags they started in.
Prohibited Items: Please no dirty, broken, or dangerous items are allowed.

Items initially seeded in the chest include stickers, clothing, USB cables, audio adapters, books, BDSM adjacent items, sunglasses, drink ware, bags, burn swag, and several other
title: MzFit’s Magical Treasure Chest (PG-13+)

Make a custom leather collar or let your inner pet shine with your own set of handmade ears. As it turns out, leather headbands and ears are suuuper comfy and cute AF.

Fun for all skill levels. Children under 16 will need adult assistance. Materials provided. Vegan leather not provided.

The Glory Hole is a playful boutique and gathering space dedicated to radical self-expression, transformation, confidence, and fun. Stop by to explore a constantly evolving collection of clothing, accessories, costumes, sparkle, fur, fringe, sunglasses, hats, and whatever else seems right in the moment.  

Try something on. Reinvent yourself for the night. Become a disco cowboy. A desert socialite. A sequined cryptid. Or just find the one ridiculous accessory that somehow completes your entire weekend.  

We’ll also be serving:  

Mouthmosas — fresh mimosas delivered directly into your mouth.  

Breakfast Shots — cereal, horchata, and optional booze in one surprisingly delicious shot.  

The Glory Hole is equal parts boutique, lounge, social hub, and midday bad decision support center. Come hang out, dress up, laugh with strangers, and leave feeling just a little more spectacular than when you arrived.

description:
Ready to learn how to Boot, Scoot, and Boogie? 
Welcome to the world of line and swing dancing! 
No dance experience needed: completely beginner friendly (and so much fun!) 
Learn a couple basic swing moves that you can do with your partner on any dance floor! Country Swing is the foundation of Honky Tonkin, and such a good bonding experience. We’ll teach you the basics (and maybe a few fun tricks!) 
Line dancing is a really fun confidence booster, and really fun once you get the hang of it! We’ll be teaching a few completely beginner dances to get the feel of the steps and we’ll teach you all the terminology needed. 
Dances on the list: 
Flip a Quarter (Heads Carolina)
Boot Scootin Boogie 
Miles on It
Crank it Up (contra!)
Texas Time
Lonely drum
Good Time and more if requested!
The best part of both is no previous dance experience required! We are so excited, and hope you join us!
title: Swing Dance with Gena!

description: Learn how to spin buugeng (s-staffs) beautifully through this introductory workshop! Buugeng sets available for use during the class. We’ll go over the three building blocks of buugeng spinning, including the wrist motion used in moves like the two beat weave and the wrist motion used in reels. We’ll cover single and double handed weaves in both the forward and backward directions, and we’ll get an introduction to the many types of reels that are possible with buugeng. We’ll touch on the third building block - finger spinning and the endless possibilities it can unlock. Participants will learn how to spin buugeng gently in order to maximize the optical effects of the buugeng. Come play!
title: Buuginner Buugeng

description:
Instructor - Ayla, she/her

In a world howling with tumultuous noise, wouldn’t you like to walk a quieter path? The secret language of hands. Haven’t you always wondered? In this workshop, I will introduce you to the foundational basics of American Sign Language. Together, we’ll learn how to spell our own names, introduce ourselves to people, and some important phrases to get by. We’ll also touch upon some cultural topics. Ayla was born Deaf and has over 12 years of experience teaching ASL at the community college level. The instructional portion of this workshop will be presented mostly in signs and gestures. Bring your bodies, not your voices, and let’s let our hands do the talking.

IMPORTANT LOCATION INFO: We are located at the SOAK Welcome Sign at the end of Forest Entry closest to where you drive in. Most map resources are incorrect about our placement. Don’t let that stop you!
title: Fingering 101: a Gentle Introduction to ASL

description:
Instructor - Dillon, he/him

Did you attend "Fingering 101"? Excellent! You might have learned enough ASL to follow along with this workshop! If not, don't worry, I'm a deaf engineer, and I'll have a student interpreter with me, so I can finally talk at my normal pace of 500 words a second. 

We're diving into the delightfully disruptive world of DIY haptics, where cheap tech gives you expensive pleasure. The motorized rumbling and buzzing of haptics are a useful and effective sensation for Deaf people to interact with the hearing world, among other uses.

We'll cover all the basics: sourcing actuators that will really move you (like Eccentric Rotating Mass (ERMs) and Linear Resonant Actuators (LRAs)), optimizing prices using microcontrollers that costs less than a can of coffee, and designing your own device for maximum impact. Come learn how to build your own custom vibrator for fun and communication on the cheap!
title: Good Vibrations: Getting Off on Cheap Hardware

description:
As the night tide rises, it may wash you to rocky shores where an ancient creature sleeps. All that remains is a turtle shell and two sleeping eggs; who knows what may slumber within them. Feel yourself grow smaller as you approach this massive Cretaceous relic of the deep past. Crawl inside and grow in size, it’s a tiny cinema! Inside the hollow of the shell is a cozy sanctuary where the festival’s roar fades into the rhythmic hum of the universe. Settle into the landscape of blankets and pillows, gaze up at the screen, and contemplate the mystery and beauty of the natural world. Turtles are radically self-reliant, you know, carrying their shelter on their back. So breathe deeply, you’re home. And once you've caught your breath, you'll emerge, like a hatchling, back into the neon sea. 

Always wanted to ride a giant turtle? Climb aboard and enjoy the view!

Fire breathers and specialty props must check in with our safety team before performing. Fuel donations gratefully accepted. All participants must follow posted fire circle guidelines and safety team instructions.

It's recommended to keep your story to 10 mins or less in order to make space for others, but it's not a hard and fast rule. Hours may vary from the schedule set here, if we're not home, please swing by again later on.  If the fire is lit, you are welcome to stop by.

It is rumored that it can only appear in a potent nexus of chaotic creation energy, where the bookshop manifests from the ether at the stroke of midnight, offering a curated collection of enchanted* tomes. Whether you’re looking for a manual on advanced levitation or just a good cosmic mystery, grab a lantern and step inside before the clock strikes two and the shop dissolves back into the starlight.

*Enchanted tomes are use-at-your-own-risk. The librarians strongly encourage the uninitiated to select only from the enchant-ing books. Not sure which is which? Well, you're definitely not a witch! (Wordplay welcomed.)

description:
Open throughout the event. Look for the starburst lights (which are not for taking!)
 Did you forget that small something that you need? We might have it! This is a whimsical, resource-sharing project that is operated on the honor system, and designed to foster community kindness, sustainability, and mutual support. Take what you need, leave what you can.

Take What You Need: Items are free to take, but please limit to just a couple of items per visit
Leave What You Can: Contributors are encouraged to leave clean, working items/swag - this helps keep things going!
Keep it Neatly Stocked: Please keep the chest tidy, after exploring - refolding clothing or putting items back in bags they started in.
Prohibited Items: Please no dirty, broken, or dangerous items are allowed.

Items initially seeded in the chest include stickers, clothing, USB cables, audio adapters, books, BDSM adjacent items, sunglasses, drink ware, bags, burn swag, and several other
title: MzFit’s Magical Treasure Chest (PG-13+)

description:
Unicorns! Join us for an ecstatic dance journey in the key of horny. Move in whatever way feels good in your body as we move along a musical journey that begins with slow, grounding tracks and then moves into some dynamic herd-shaking grooves. Set-list includes some explicit tracks that may not be suitable for children, even your feral ones.

Warm-up: 11:00
Opening circle: 11:15
Closing circle: 12:45

Missed the opening circle? Here's an important cheat-sheet:

1) Non-Verbal Container. Except when needed for safety, take your verbal conversations outside of the dance space. Same with phones.

2) Consent. Unicorns sometimes dance together, but only with the explicit consent from prospective dance partners.

3) Nonjudgment. Especially toward yourself. Move how your body wants to move (or not move).
title: Ecstatic Prance

You win bonus points if you come dressed as your favorite Monty Python character and/or your favorite small to medium-sized perennial woody plant.

description:
The Glory Hole is a playful boutique and gathering space dedicated to radical self-expression, transformation, confidence, and fun. Stop by to explore a constantly evolving collection of clothing, accessories, costumes, sparkle, fur, fringe, sunglasses, hats, and whatever else seems right in the moment.  

Try something on. Reinvent yourself for the night. Become a disco cowboy. A desert socialite. A sequined cryptid. Or just find the one ridiculous accessory that somehow completes your entire weekend.  

We’ll also be serving:  

Mouthmosas — fresh mimosas delivered directly into your mouth.  

Breakfast Shots — cereal, horchata, and optional booze in one surprisingly delicious shot.  

The Glory Hole is equal parts boutique, lounge, social hub, and midday bad decision support center. Come hang out, dress up, laugh with strangers, and leave feeling just a little more spectacular than when you arrived.

Serving the immeasurable.

We deal in unreasonable quantities:

~ Gallons of calm in a single breath
~ Miles of shiver from one small touch
~ A whole ocean, poured neatly into your nervous system
~Forests of mint, released in a moment

Be shrunk. Be stretched. Be gently  mismeasured.

description:
Thursday - Sunday DanceSafe will have volunteers available for substance checking between hours of 1pm-5pm, we will post any extra hours volunteers will be available on a white board by our booth.

The booth of supplies will be stocked 24/7 though so that people can come get supplies as needed throughout the duration of SOAK.  Stop by our booth at any time to pick up harm reduction supplies such as safer sex supplies, consent literature, safer use kits, drug education and literature, earplugs, fentanyl test strips and more.
Our volunteers will be around the listed times to provide substance checking as well as helping answer any questions you may have about harm reduction and safer use.
title: DanceSafe Substance Verification

description:
Instructor - Claire (she/her)

Have fun learning about radical activist hardware and technology you usually never hear about. Like mesh radio without all the useless jargon, and cyberdecks and the epic current solarpunk femme queer movement around them. Also offgrid prep and what tech you can add to it. How e-waste, emotions, relationships, art and perspective intersect with all of it. And more.

It’s really just an excuse to infodump some of the coolest things I know to the people I want to share this with most, the femmes, queers, BIPOC babes. The nervous ones. The non-technical but curious artists. Come learn.

Your facilitator will be none other than Claire, self taught professional hardware hacker and engineer, prolific artist, aggressive info sharer, LED wizard, tech gatekeeping demolisher, and lead at There U Glow.

IMPORTANT LOCATION INFO: We are located at the SOAK Welcome Sign at the end of Forest Entry closest to where you drive in. Most map resources are incorrect about our

description:
As the night tide rises, it may wash you to rocky shores where an ancient creature sleeps. All that remains is a turtle shell and two sleeping eggs; who knows what may slumber within them. Feel yourself grow smaller as you approach this massive Cretaceous relic of the deep past. Crawl inside and grow in size, it’s a tiny cinema! Inside the hollow of the shell is a cozy sanctuary where the festival’s roar fades into the rhythmic hum of the universe. Settle into the landscape of blankets and pillows, gaze up at the screen, and contemplate the mystery and beauty of the natural world. Turtles are radically self-reliant, you know, carrying their shelter on their back. So breathe deeply, you’re home. And once you've caught your breath, you'll emerge, like a hatchling, back into the neon sea. 

Always wanted to ride a giant turtle? Climb aboard and enjoy the view!

You gotta go up to get down!  V,S,M is located at the top of the Upper Bowl. And remember adventuring rule #1: Don't split the (dance) party!

description:
Prismatic Spray, 7th level evocation. Casting time: 1 action. Range: Self. Target: Each creature in a 60-ft cone. Components: V,S. Duration: Instantaneous. Classes: Bard, Sorcerer, Wizard, Occultist. 

Will you make your Dexterity saving throw, or get caught by the spell?

description: Join the Cats in Space Fire Circle after dark in an ancient ritual of fire that transcends time and space. Bring a blanket or camp chair to enjoy the show. Fire performers welcome - we provide experienced fire safeties on duty all night, fuel depot, and a dedicated performance space (bring your own props). Fire breathers and specialty props must check in with our safety team before performing. Fuel donations gratefully accepted. All participants must follow posted fire circle guidelines and safety team instructions.
title: Fire Circle

description: Restore your HP with a warm bowl of Midnight Miso. It’s the perfect, simple potion to fortify you for the next adventure. The miso will be available starting at 10:30pm, until it's gone. Vegan and gluten-free!
title: Midnight Miso

description: It came from somewhere outside the known spectrum. It does not communicate in words. It does not need to. Heavy electro and bass frequencies give you a creeping feeling you can't quite place. This is not a warning.
title: The Color Out of Bass w/Tock the Watchdog

description: Hear colors! See sounds! Come watch the Synthesthesia House Band perform on the light sculptures!
title: Synthesthesia House Band

description: We will be servings fresh margaritas to quench your  thirst as well as other canned alcoholic drinks. We do have a small stash of non-alcoholic drinks as well.

description:
It's a May Day, let's have a parade! Join the Party Vanguard! We will parade through SOAK from Astro Shack Java Joint in the Meadow to Undisclosed Location over at Riverside to have our Fully Automated Luxury Queer Space Communist Party. Joyful Noises and Revolutionary Slogans Encouraged!

We will then have music, Black Russians (never White Russians for historical reasons) and cold brew coffee as we plot revolution in our Temporary Autonomus Zone.
title: Fully Automated Luxury Queer Space Communist Parade and Party

description:
Warm Snuggles is a safe place to relax and feel safe.

With pillows and things to sit on one can Catch a breath and make new friends.
title: Safe place to relax

description: Take a set, or lay back on the hammocks, and swings
title: Take a load off

description: Want to send a message to a friend? Lover? Mysterious stranger with a cool aura? Come visit Message in a Bottle, SOAK's three-dimensional missed connections. Paper and writing utensils are provided for message-making, once your message is complete, roll it up and place it in an empty bottle. Write the recipient's name and/or description on the bottle tag and leave for them to find the next time they wander by. Check back in throughout the weekend to see if anything has been left for you!

description: Come see us at the Wizard Bag to receive a silly 'task' to add some frivolous whimsy to your day!

description: Tune in for official news and information about SOAK, local DJ sets, and music at all hours of the day.  We'll be broadcasting during event build and through event close.
title: SOAK Information Radio 88.1FM

description:
Under the Green Lantern you'll find the Green
Fairy serving Absinthe and side quests.
Completed side quests will be rewarded with magical potions for your journey!
title: Green Lantern: Absinthe & Side Quests

description: This tranquil oasis will be full to the brim with dried beans. Relax as you float effortlessly on a sea of buoyant beans. Please note, life guards will be on duty to keep you safe and Bean Safety Agents will be checking each patron before exiting the pool area to ensure everyone de-bean properly.

description: It's back! We've hidden GIGANTIC beans all around SOAK. They're just waiting for you to find! How to play: 1) Explore the SOAK universe while keeping your eyes peeled and your senses sharp! 2) Find a GIANT bean and take this keepsake home with you! No need to return to us. This year we have hidden several bean varieties including Great Northern, Colorful, smaller mutant beans and a few magic beans from last year. 3) Find another bean? Please leave it be so others can discover and participate in the hunt.  The beans will be hidden by 3pm on Friday. All unfound beans will be picked up by end of day Sunday. Happy hunting!

description: This is a marble race track like no one has ever seen before! Watch as your selected marble races through Soak and Burning Man themed lands such as Black Rock City, Porta Potty Alley, and Sparkle Pony Lane - just to name a few! The track will be available most of the day/night but at certain times (when we are feeling motivated) we will be handing out epic prizes to the winners. Marbles will be provided.

other_location: SPK 2.0 at the far back left corner of the Mezzanine

description: Best viewed at night, our cult has captured sea life shapes of all sizes which can be viewed through the distorted lens of a giant collide-us-scope.

description:
Open throughout the event. Look for the starburst lights (which are not for taking!)
 Did you forget that small something that you need? We might have it! This is a whimsical, resource-sharing project that is operated on the honor system, and designed to foster community kindness, sustainability, and mutual support. Take what you need, leave what you can.

Take What You Need: Items are free to take, but please limit to just a couple of items per visit
Leave What You Can: Contributors are encouraged to leave clean, working items/swag - this helps keep things going!
Keep it Neatly Stocked: Please keep the chest tidy, after exploring - refolding clothing or putting items back in bags they started in.
Prohibited Items: Please no dirty, broken, or dangerous items are allowed.

Items initially seeded in the chest include stickers, clothing, USB cables, audio adapters, books, BDSM adjacent items, sunglasses, drink ware, bags, burn swag, and several other

description: Tired of wandering the woods? Step inside. We're not just a camp, we're a destination for the sole. Come to Only Feet for a foot themed coloring book session, we've got the pages, the pens and the shade, you just need to bring the creativity. Color a page to take home or add your masterpiece to our "Gallery of Toes" hanging in the camp. Lets make this the most colorful walk in the woods yet.

description: When you pass by The Shrubbery, please pause a moment and visit our decorative shrub. Whether you are stopping at our camp to get a drink or just walking past please join in with our art project.  There are fuzzy leaves and colorful flowers available. You might add a pin or item of your own.  Take a selfie or give the shrub a hug (it likes hugs).  Help create something uniquely beautiful together at SOAK.

This is a first time collaboration between two long time friends and members of the silly club, Maggi and Anna. It is a project of creativity and radical self expression. We are very excited to share it with our SOAK community!

Ask for Jared

Our comfy, chill lockpicking lounge is open all day and night for you to teach yourself lockpicking, mess around or just watch people try. We will provide books tools and resources to learn from, but we do not actively teach this skill. Radical self-reliance!

IMPORTANT LOCATION INFO: We are located at the SOAK Welcome Sign at the end of Forest Entry closest to where you drive in. Most map resources are incorrect about our placement. Don’t let that stop you!

Dance into the night and through sunrise with Wild Things! We're bringing house music to this dance party. We'll keep the vibes going strong into sunrise by transitioning to silent disco at 3 am with bubbles to share to toast at sunrise - then close out a night full of love! 

We hope you can join us! This event is from 10 pm until 6 am.

10-11 pm - Rob Noble
11 pm - midnight - Drew Harris midnight-1 am - Ms Match

The hangout for quasi-legal alien beatniks, mutant cyborg gear heads, trans-solar scooter gangs, radical anarchist raypunks, neuroatypical geeks, freaked-out psychonauts, and caffeine fiends of every stripe.

Coffee, pancakes, bacon, buds, and good conversation.

Do you need a Caffè Corretto from Italy, or perhaps a Carajillo (in either Spanish or Mexican style), or maybe even a Karsk from Norway?

Try these drinks as well as many others from around the world every morning of SOAK.

From British emulators to retromodern country and northwestern sounds, it’s music for the hour when the sun starts warming your tent, stove-tops fire up, coffee drips, and camp slowly comes to life.

DJ Yoon, the host of this program, is naturally a night owl who isn’t typically ambulant before noon. Out at SOAK, he can't sleep in so much... This is an eclectic 1-hour radio program for those of you who tend to brew up coffee and eat breakfast well after your peers have already had theirs. It is presented in a classic “AM-Gold” fashion complete with weather updates, local news, and a traffic report.

We reprinted these cards on small cardstock and assembled 12 unique decks for you to try out. We will teach you how to play and no experience with Arctic Cards or Magic is necessary. Enjoy light snacks and alcoholic or non-alcoholic drinks in a provided drinking vessel inspired by one of the cards.

Something for all the senses, including (but not limited to) head scritchies, essential oil aroma therapy, manicures, feather tickles, live ASMR, and more!

camp: The Glory Hole - A Spectacular Boutique
description:
The Glory Hole is a playful boutique and gathering space dedicated to radical self-expression, transformation, confidence, and fun. Stop by to explore a constantly evolving collection of clothing, accessories, costumes, sparkle, fur, fringe, sunglasses, hats, and whatever else seems right in the moment.  

Try something on. Reinvent yourself for the night. Become a disco cowboy. A desert socialite. A sequined cryptid. Or just find the one ridiculous accessory that somehow completes your entire weekend.  

We’ll also be serving:  

Mouthmosas — fresh mimosas delivered directly into your mouth.  

Breakfast Shots — cereal, horchata, and optional booze in one surprisingly delicious shot.  

The Glory Hole is equal parts boutique, lounge, social hub, and midday bad decision support center. Come hang out, dress up, laugh with strangers, and leave feeling just a little more spectacular than when you arrived.

description:

While music reaches peak intensity during some parts of the journey, the set starts and ends with slow, grounding tracks. Come reconnect to your body, the dance floor, and other dancers after "burn" night, moving in whatever ways call to you!

Opening circle: 12:30
Closing circle: 2:00

Bare feet preferred!

description:

Partake in a satirical communion ceremony, learn how monkeys came from Africa to South America, listen to a shockingly vulnerable sermon that may or may not include a mini math lecture, and sit in community for something somewhere between Quaker unplanned worship and a support group.

"That was the coolest church service I've ever been at."

description: LISTEN UP, BECAUSE THIS IS THE MOST IMPORTANT THING YOU WILL READ TODAY AND POSSIBLY EVER. You are going to come to this workshop and you are going to build a GIANT BUBBLE WAND with your own two beautiful hands. Yes, YOU. We're talking enormous, sky-swallowing, dog-startling bubbles that will make strangers weep with awe. Want a smaller wand too? GREAT, we're doing those as well because we are GENEROUS and UNHINGED. Supplies are provided. The sacred bubble solution recipe will be revealed — I will teach you the formula personally like some kind of soap alchemist passing down forbidden knowledge. The first half is building, the second half is absolutely losing your mind in a field watching iridescent orbs the size of a golden retriever float toward the sun. This is for kids. This is for adults. This is for anyone who has ever looked at a bubble and thought "I need that to be bigger and I need it NOW." If you have forgotten what joy feels like, we will remind you.

The booth of supplies will be stocked 24/7 though so that people can come get supplies as needed throughout the duration of SOAK. description:
Instructor - Carrie Sundra (she/her)

Do you vaguely know what a circuit board is, but not how it works or how it’s constructed?  Would you like to have someone explain this and the jargon around them?  Are you mystified by how someone can seemingly just pull a fresh circuit board design out of thin air, show the physical board to you, and be like “I made this!”?  Well come on over!  Carrie is an engineer and maker (and formerly owned Alpenglow Industries) with 25+ years of experience making circuit boards and it’s her default art form for rage-making and political commentary.  She loves to break down gatekeeping and share her knowledge with anyone who’s curious.

IMPORTANT LOCATION INFO: We are located at the SOAK Welcome Sign at the end of Forest Entry closest to where you drive in. Most map resources are incorrect about our placement. Don’t let that stop you!
title: What the hell is a circuit board and what do you mean I can just make one?

description:
Sadly or mercifully, all things must come to an end… and so too must your sense of disassociation from the default.

We invite you to partake in the sacred ritual of the Dunkaroo—a full-body, multi-sensory immersion designed to realign your chakras, awaken your nervous system, and return you firmly to the present moment as you prepare for re-entry.

This carefully curated wellness journey includes:
- A brisk cryogenic plunge to invigorate the spirit
- A percussive somatic adjustment to restore balance
- A medicinal libation to ease integration

Please bring a canned beverage as your offering to the collective—whether as tribute, tithe, or simply something that no longer sparks joy.

Featuring live vibrational accompaniment by:
Megaphonic Supersonic Incredible Peanut Gallery

Come cleanse. Come center. Come back different.

description:
Class/Workshop name: Rope Flow: tension, movement, and connection 

In this circus-inspired, adult-oriented workshop, we explore rope as a flow art: blending rhythm, movement, and connection into a practice that’s meditative and electric.  We will take rope handling techniques from shibari and use touch, timing, and nonverbal language to create expressive and organic dance sequences. We will explore sensations like power, surrender, and eroticism, all grounded in consent and curiosity.

Bring a 28-30ft piece of rope if you have one (I will have some loaners). No prior rope experience is necessary.  A tying partner is great but many of the exercises can also be labbed solo.
title: Rope Flow: tension, movement, and connection

description: Do you ever feel like you’re crawling as fast as you can through the dust, headed straight into the vast, deep unknown? Embrace that cosmic scramble at SOAK’s first-ever Sea Turtle Release! That's right, nature’s most chaotic migration is coming to the playa. Don your best turtle gear and join us at the Colossal Cretaceous Compact Cinema for our very first hatchling release. Once the nest breaks, it’s a high-stakes game of freeze-tag: Turtles must race to the ocean…..themed bar at Firestarter while dodging hungry seagulls and relying on a passionate environmentalist to unfreeze them along the way. Make it to the ocean and join our “Out to Sea” turtle farewell party. Will you reach the deep blue, or end up as bird food? Come find out!

As pirates, we arrrr keenly aware o’ our weakness for all things shiny and priceless! So hoist yer courage and join us for a night o’ Blackjack and Booty Huntin’. Test yer luck, wager yer trinkets, and mayhap walk away with more treasure than yer little pirate heart ever dreamed of.

Come play, specially  ye scallywags that lost ye precious items during our pirate outings!

description: Hoist yer tankards high, ye scurvy lot! Step into our grog‑soaked haven where the rum runs deep, the spirits stay lively, and every pirate—seasoned or fresh off the docks—can wet their whistle without worry o’ coin or consequence.

description:
What does disappointment taste like? Is it salty, sweet, just a cup of ice, a cement mixer, or surprisingly better than you expected? Each hand crafted beverage is lovingly crafted to ensure maximum disappointment (either by the patron, bartender, or both). We offer alcoholic and non alcoholic options. We are happy to provide disappointment to adults and children alike, just be sure to bring a cup (or cup adjacent item).

We'll either be at home base (just inside the mezzanine) or in our mobile form at the base of the not-burn gathering.
title: Disappointing Cocktail Hour

description:

description:
Every evening right after magic hour when it falls dark we will be gifting Fire Portraits with our fire poofer! Come dressed up in your finest costumes! 
Solo or with your entire camp; Luna and Oz will gift you a
"Magical Fire-Poofing Portrait Experience". 
Along with our fire portraits, Camp FYFA resident DJ Ant-Eye will be taking you on an underground main stage Burning Man Vibe set for your dancing pleasures.

description: Instead of experiencing our catharsis through Temple burn this year, we are SOAKing it. Come by and visit our altar where a vessel of healing waters awaits you and anything you wish to release. Write on the dissolving paper, liberate it to the water and watch it melt away.

description: Whenever there is a Hang Loose member available and willing, please come join us for some tea and relax. Iced tea during the day, hot tea to warm you up at night. Hours will vary throughout each day.

description: If you follow that path into the sunset you may find yourself on the happy trail. A place to stop and take a moment to consider What Haunts You. As you reflect, your host Time Being, will be spinning her final set of SOAK 2026. Stop by for an all vinyl set before you drift off into the darkness.

description:
Team Cellographic presents the Oracle of Mouses. We bring with us, Soiree, a representative from the Muse dimension. She entertains with her siren's song, the interdimensional broadcast of Love Muse Oracle TV (lmoTV), a retro futuristic network of nostalgic visions. 
Her media has lured her audience of mouses, created by SOAK folk like you. The Mouses are the Masses. Perhaps you are one of them?

description:

Camp DJs will be playing B2B keeping the vibes downtempo and deep house.
title: Creatures of Leisure

title: Latin Lovers (Dance Party)

description:
As the night tide rises, it may wash you to rocky shores where an ancient creature sleeps. All that remains is a turtle shell and two sleeping eggs; who knows what may slumber within them. Feel yourself grow smaller as you approach this massive Cretaceous relic of the deep past. Crawl inside and grow in size, it’s a tiny cinema! Inside the hollow of the shell is a cozy sanctuary where the festival’s roar fades into the rhythmic hum of the universe. Settle into the landscape of blankets and pillows, gaze up at the screen, and contemplate the mystery and beauty of the natural world. Turtles are radically self-reliant, you know, carrying their shelter on their back. So breathe deeply, you’re home. And once you've caught your breath, you'll emerge, like a hatchling, back into the neon sea. 

Always wanted to ride a giant turtle? Climb aboard and enjoy the view!
title: Primordial Projections

description: Have you been practicing your bean karaoke skills all weekend? Well, here's your chance to prove that you're SOAK 2025's beaniest singer at our 2nd annual Beaneoke Championship. Perform your favorite song in front of our discerning panel of judges. If you perform with enough beanthusiasm, we might even award you a prize. Limited to the first 10 entrants. Each song can only be requested once, so come early to reserve your preferred tune. Sign ups will be open all day Sunday so come on by and snag your spot! Party starts at 9 pm!
title: Beaneoke Championships

description: We all have to load up in the morning and head out. That doesn't mean we can't get together one last time. Come join us for one last drink, laugh, or story before we leave this magical place. We start at 9 and go til who knows when.

description:
Each night, we host a propane fire pit  front and center at UE for folks to warm their bones and hearts. We invite you to come by and share a story, or receive stories that others may share. There will be a different theme for each night to help inspire and focus your sharing. It's recommended to keep your story to 10 mins or less in order to make space for others, but it's not a hard and fast rule. Hours may vary from the schedule set here, if we're not home, please swing by again later on.  If the fire is lit, you are welcome to stop by.
Thursday (after dusk):
Friday (after 10pm):
Saturday (after the not-Burn):
Sunday (after Temple):
title: Fireside Storytelling

description:
After darkness falls, we project on our outdoor movie screen bizarre, strange, and occasionally WTF content designed to make you you ask if these woods are safe.

Bring a camp chair or take off your shoes and climb in the Shark Pit(tm) to enjoy the cinematic weirdness.

No shoes, drinks, food or wrestling in the Shark Pit.

description:
This is your last chance to let your freak flags fly at Krampusberg for this year.  So, come fill yourself with our spirits, as we close the veil for another year at Soak.  

The goats will  sing at Midnight.
title: Tempelnacht

description:
While we cannot have our traditional wood burn, we are re-imagining our end-of-event ritual. On Sunday evening, in place of and at the time of our typical burn, we will be hosting an alternative ceremony: a "Cleansing by Water and Light." We will gather as we typically would for the burn for a ceremony that we hope will give a sense of catharsis, community, and continuity for all. 
Water soluble paper will be provided at the Temple throughout the event and the evening following the ceremony for those who want to see their offerings resolved before leaving SOAK. Following the gathering, the Temple will remain open through the night, and participants are welcome to add more offerings to be dissolved and released.

Gather: 9:30pm. Ceremony start: 10pm
title: Cleansing by Water and Light: Temple Ceremony

description: Join the Cats in Space Fire Circle after dark in an ancient ritual of fire that transcends time and space. Bring a blanket or camp chair to enjoy the show. Fire performers welcome - we provide experienced fire safeties on duty all night, fuel depot, and a dedicated performance space (bring your own props). Fire breathers and specialty props must check in with our safety team before performing. Fuel donations gratefully accepted. All participants must follow posted fire circle guidelines and safety team instructions.

description:
You've heard of Irish Coffee, right? We'd like to bring to you alcoholic (and NA, should you prefer) caffeinated concoctions from cultures around the world!

Do you need a Caffè Corretto from Italy, or perhaps a Carajillo (in either Spanish or Mexican style), or maybe even a Karsk from Norway?

Try these drinks as well as many others from around the world every morning of SOAK.
